8    24    12    36    18    54

∴ ? = 36

(d) Here, the pattern is as follows
Divide by 2, multiple by 3 and the same process is repeated
48 ÷ 2 = 24 ; 24 × 3 = 72
72 ÷ 2 = 36 ; 36 × 3 = 108
108 ÷ 2 = 54
∴ ? = 54

(c) Keep subtracting the next larger perfect cube i.e., 1, 8, 27, 64, 125, 216, 343
∴ ? = 434 - 343 = 91

(b) The given series is the series of prime numbers.
3,5,7,11,13,17,19,23
∴ ? = 11

(b) The sequence is 7¹, 6², 5³, 4⁴, 3⁵, 2⁶, 1⁷
∴ ? = 3⁵ = 9

(c) The pattern of series is as follows
4    18    48    100    180    294
2²×1  3²×2  4²×3  5²×4  6²×5  7²×6
∴ ? = 48

(d) The pattern of series is as follows
4×2+1=9; 9×2+2=20
20×2+3=43; 43×2+4=90
∴ ? = 90

(b) The pattern is as follows
+1    +1    +1
2    29    38    47    56
-1    -1    -1
So, the correct alternative is 56.
(c) There are three alternative series 2, 3, 4, ... (consecutive numbers), 9, 8, 7 ... (consecutive number in descending order) and 23, 25, 27, ... Hence, 7 will come in place of the missing number.

(b) The pattern is as follows
21
6    7    9    13    26    31    69
+1  +2  +4  +8  +16  +32
Hence, 21 should come in place of 26 in the series.

(b) Series moves from the right end with addition of 0, 3, 8, 15, 24 and 35 i.e., with a difference of numbers, which are one less than the square of successive natural numbers. Therefore, number 54 should be replaced by 52.

(b) The pattern is as follows
82
22    37    52    67    84    97
+15  +15  +15  +15  +15
So, 84 is the incorrect term, it should be 82.
(b) In rest of the numbers, one digit is the square of the rest part of the number.

(c) The pattern is as follows
10→(1)²(0)², 41→(2)²(1)², 94→(3)²(2)²,
2516→(5)²(4)², 3625→(6)²(5)², 4936→(7)²(6)²
but in 1624 it is not so.

(d) The pattern is as follows
193
4    7    13    25    49    97    153
+3  +6  +12  +24  +48  +96
So, 153 is the wrong term in the series
